notes: Skill achieves 100% pass rate vs 42% baseline across all three full-flow evals
The 4 assertions that always fail for baseline are: marker insertion, FACT-CHECKS.md update, two-tier report structure, and fix workflow — all behaviors the skill uniquely provides
Error detection and fix application both pass for baseline (assertions 1–3 in each eval) — the skill's value is entirely in systematic structure and the persistent tracker
Race condition: parallel agents sharing source files caused some baseline runs to compete with with-skill runs for the same file; in all cases the final file state was correct

MOSFETs with-skill found an additional real error not injected: wrong failure mechanism attribution (gate oxide vs drain-body avalanche) — shows the calibration table catches errors beyond the obvious
Time overhead is substantial in iteration 2: with-skill averages 183s vs 93s without (2× slower), reflecting the full flow: apply fixes + insert markers + update tracker
Token overhead also larger: +12,784 tokens per run on average, for the full rewrite + marker insertion + FACT-CHECKS.md work
